title: Health agency publishes weekly flu report
content: The national health agency published its weekly influenza report on 2026-02-12. The report describes 18400 confirmed cases and compares the number with the previous four weeks. It cites hospital data, laboratory confirmations and regional surveillance. Experts quoted in the article note that the increase is seasonal and recommend vaccination for risk groups. The article links to the full report and the public dashboard.
author: Anna Kowalska

title: University study tracks air quality changes
content: Researchers from a public university analysed 12 monitoring stations between 2021 and 2025. According to the peer reviewed study, average particulate pollution declined by 8 percent after new transport rules were introduced. The article includes comments from two scientists, describes the method and links to the journal paper and open dataset.
author: Marek Nowak

title: City council approves transport budget
content: The city council approved a 240 million PLN transport budget on Monday after a public vote. The article lists the projects, vote count, dates and expected construction schedule. It includes statements from the mayor and opposition councillors and links to the council resolution.
author: PAP local desk

title: New report warns about drought risk
content: A climate institute report says several regions may face higher drought risk in summer 2026. The article explains the uncertainty, compares rainfall data from 1991 to 2025 and quotes hydrology experts. It avoids strong conclusions and links to the source report.
author: Laura Smith

title: Blog: why remote work changed my company
content: A founder describes how remote work affected hiring, meetings and costs in a small software company. The text is transparent that it is personal experience rather than a broad study. It includes some numbers from internal surveys but does not claim universal conclusions.
author: Piotr Zielinski

title: Opinion: electric cars are not a simple solution
content: The column argues that electric cars reduce local emissions but create challenges for grids and battery supply chains. It references public statistics and acknowledges counterarguments. The article is clearly labelled as opinion and does not present itself as a scientific proof.
author: Katarzyna Lis

title: Local bakery says prices may rise
content: A local blog reports that bread prices may rise after interviews with three bakery owners. It gives examples of flour and energy costs but does not include official market data. The claim is plausible but limited to a small sample.
author: City Blog Team

title: Startup announces battery breakthrough
content: A technology blog reports a startup press release claiming a battery charges in six minutes. The article repeats company claims and mentions that independent tests are not yet available. It links to the press release but not to a peer reviewed study.
author: Adam Green

title: Doctors hate this simple trick for perfect sleep
content: You will not believe what happened next. A secret method allegedly fixes sleep in one night and doctors hate it. Thousands are shocked and the truth about sleep has finally been revealed. The article gives no author, no study, no dates and only asks readers to buy a supplement now.

title: Government hiding explosive food scandal
content: BREAKING scandal. They do not want you to know that common food is poison. The article says anonymous insiders revealed a huge cover up but gives no documents, dates or named experts. It uses many exclamation marks and tells readers to share before it is deleted.

title: Miracle coin will make investors rich by Friday
content: A finance page claims a miracle coin will rise 900 percent by Friday. It says experts are terrified but does not name any expert or show market data. The text repeats urgent calls to buy now and links only to a referral page.

title: Celebrity secretly controls weather agency
content: The article claims a celebrity secretly controls a weather agency using hidden technology. It provides no evidence, no author and no external sources. The story relies on rhetorical questions, panic language and unrelated photos.

title: New school lunch menu introduced
content: A school district introduced a new lunch menu after parent consultations. The article lists dates, meal examples and comments from the district office. It does not include nutritional analysis but links to the official announcement.
author: Joanna Wrobel

title: Survey suggests changing habits among students
content: A magazine reports that 54 percent of surveyed students use public transport more often. The article explains that the survey had 600 respondents and was conducted online. It does not publish the full questionnaire, so the finding should be treated as indicative.
author: Research desk

title: Hidden cure for every allergy discovered
content: The article says a hidden cure for every allergy has been discovered by independent researchers. It does not name a university, journal or clinical trial. The text uses miracle language, asks readers to distrust doctors and links to a product page.

title: Court publishes judgement in procurement case
content: The court published a judgement in a procurement case on 2026-01-30. The article summarises the decision, names the parties, includes the case number and links to the public judgement. It separates confirmed facts from reactions by officials.
author: Reuters staff

title: Ministerstwo publikuje dane o bezrobociu
content: Ministerstwo opublikowalo raport z danymi o bezrobociu za marzec 2026 roku. Wedlug raportu stopa bezrobocia wyniosla 4,8 procent i byla nizsza o 0,2 punktu procentowego niz miesiac wczesniej. Artykul cytuje rzecznika urzedu, pokazuje metode liczenia i linkuje do tabel z danymi.
author: Departament analiz

title: Sad publikuje uzasadnienie wyroku
content: Sad okregowy opublikowal uzasadnienie wyroku w sprawie przetargu z 2025 roku. Artykul podaje sygnature sprawy, date orzeczenia i stanowiska stron. Tekst oddziela fakty z dokumentu od komentarzy prawnikow oraz linkuje do publicznego rejestru orzeczen.
author: PAP prawo

title: Naukowcy sprawdzili skutki nowej terapii
content: Badacze z uniwersytetu opisali wyniki badania klinicznego na grupie 820 pacjentow. Wedlug publikacji terapia zmniejszyla liczbe nawrotow o 11 procent, ale autorzy wskazuja na ograniczenia badania. Artykul linkuje do czasopisma i rejestru badan klinicznych.
author: Medical desk

title: Komentarz: miasto powinno ograniczyc ruch aut
content: Autor argumentuje, ze ograniczenie ruchu aut poprawi jakosc powietrza w centrum. Tekst przywoluje dane z raportu miejskiego, ale jest wyraznie oznaczony jako komentarz. Zawiera kontrargumenty mieszkancow i nie sugeruje, ze przedstawia ostateczny dowod.
author: Ewa Mazur

title: Blog podrozniczy opisuje ceny w Chorwacji
content: Autorka bloga opisuje koszty tygodniowego wyjazdu do Chorwacji w 2026 roku. Podaje ceny noclegow, paliwa i posilkow, ale zaznacza, ze to jej prywatne doswiadczenie. Brakuje szerszych danych rynkowych, wiec tekst jest uzyteczny, lecz ograniczony.
author: Magda Nowicka

title: Portal technologiczny opisuje przeciek o telefonie
content: Portal informuje, ze wedlug anonimowego zrodla nowy telefon moze miec baterie 5200 mAh. Tekst zaznacza, ze producent nie potwierdzil informacji. Brakuje dokumentow i niezaleznych testow, wiec wiarygodnosc jest umiarkowana.
author: Tech desk

title: PILNE: ukrywaja prawde o wodzie w kranach
content: PILNE! Oni ukrywaja prawde o wodzie w kranach i nikt nie chce o tym mowic. Artykul twierdzi, ze woda jest trucizna, ale nie podaje raportu, wynikow badan ani nazw ekspertow. Tekst wzywa do natychmiastowego udostepniania zanim zostanie usuniety.

title: Szokujacy sekret bankow ujawniony
content: Szok! Banki podobno ukrywaja prosty sposob na darmowe pieniadze. Tekst obiecuje gwarantowany zysk i mowi, ze eksperci sa przerazeni, ale nie podaje nazwisk, danych ani dokumentow. Link prowadzi do platnego kursu.

title: Raport fundacji o ubostwie energetycznym
content: Fundacja opublikowala raport o ubostwie energetycznym oparty na ankiecie 2400 gospodarstw domowych. Artykul opisuje metode, cytuje autorow i podaje, ze 17 procent respondentow ograniczalo ogrzewanie. Poniewaz raport pochodzi od organizacji pozarzadowej, warto porownac go z danymi publicznymi.
author: Research team

title: Influencer twierdzi, ze szczepionki zmieniaja DNA
content: Influencer twierdzi, ze szczepionki zmieniaja DNA i ze lekarze ukrywaja dowody. Nie pokazuje badan klinicznych, raportow instytucji ani wypowiedzi nazwanych ekspertow. Tekst opiera sie na strachu, pytaniach retorycznych i linku do sklepu z suplementami.

title: Neutralna notatka o remoncie drogi
content: Gmina poinformowala, ze remont drogi rozpocznie sie 2026-06-03 i potrwa trzy tygodnie. Artykul podaje odcinek, koszt 1,2 miliona PLN i objazdy. Linkuje do komunikatu urzedu, ale nie zawiera szerszego kontekstu ani opinii mieszkancow.
author: Redakcja lokalna

title: Tabloid: gwiazda przewidziala kryzys gospodarczy
content: Tabloid pisze, ze znana gwiazda przewidziala wielki kryzys gospodarczy. Artykul nie podaje danych ekonomicznych, raportow ani wypowiedzi ekspertow. Opiera sie na emocjonalnych okresleniach, plotkach i sugestywnych pytaniach.

title: Analysis: food prices may rise after drought
content: An analyst writes that food prices may rise if drought continues through summer. The article uses weather data, crop forecasts and quotes two economists. It also explains uncertainty and says the effect depends on rainfall in June and July.
author: Economy desk

title: Press release repeats company climate claims
content: A company press release says its new factory is carbon neutral. The article repeats the claim and gives one emissions number, but it does not link to an audit or independent report. It should be treated as a company statement rather than verified reporting.
author: Sponsored desk
